name: api-skill
capabilities: Provides a minimal YAML frontmatter structure for skill metadata
Serves as a starting template for custom API skill development
Includes basic skill configuration fields like name and description
Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code platforms
author: 0xSero
securitySummary: This is a minimal skill template containing only metadata and a simple prompt placeholder. All static findings are false positives from pattern matching on JSON field names and YAML frontmatter. No executable code, network operations, filesystem access, or cryptographic functions present. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: This minimal skill template provides a foundation for creating custom API integration prompts. It enables developers to extend Claude with specialized API interaction patterns for their specific use cases.
limitations: Contains minimal implementation with only a placeholder prompt
Requires customization and expansion for production use
No built-in API request handling or error management
Does not include example prompts or detailed documentation

useCases.title: Custom Skill Development
useCases.description: Use this template as a starting point for building custom API skills tailored to specific integration needs or proprietary APIs.

useCases.title: API Skill Prototyping
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a new API skill structure to experiment with prompt patterns and skill configurations before full implementation.

useCases.targetUser: New skill developers learning the framework
useCases.title: Learning Skill Structure
useCases.description: Study the minimal required components of a Claude skill to understand the basic metadata and configuration format.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Template Usage
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the structure of this API skill template

promptTemplates.title: Extend Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me extend this template to add API request handling for {service_name} with {authentication_type} authentication
promptTemplates.scenario: Customizing the template for specific APIs

promptTemplates.title: Add Prompt Examples
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate example prompts for this skill that demonstrate {api_operation} operations with {data_format} responses
promptTemplates.scenario: Expanding the skill with concrete examples

promptTemplates.title: Complete Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Transform this template into a full API skill for {api_provider} that handles {endpoints_list} with proper error handling and validation
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a production-ready skill from the template

bestPractices: Customize the description and name fields to clearly identify your specific API integration
Add comprehensive prompt examples that demonstrate the full range of API operations
Include authentication and error handling guidance in your skill prompts
antiPatterns: Using the template without customization in production environments
Hardcoding API credentials or tokens directly in skill files
Creating overly generic skills that do not provide specific API guidance

faq.question: What is this skill template for?
faq.answer: This is a minimal starting template for developers who want to create custom API skills for Claude. It provides the basic structure needed to build more complex integrations.

faq.question: Can I use this template as-is?
faq.answer: No. This template requires customization with your specific API details, prompts, and integration logic before it provides meaningful functionality.

faq.question: Which AI tools work with this template?
faq.answer: This template is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The skill format follows standard conventions that work across these platforms.

faq.question: How do I add my API details?
faq.answer: Edit the SKILL.md file to include your API endpoints, authentication methods, request formats, and example interactions in the prompt content area.

faq.question: Does this template make actual API calls?
faq.answer: No. This is only a template structure. You must add implementation details and the AI tool must have appropriate permissions to execute API requests.

faq.question: What should I change first?
faq.answer: Start by updating the name and description fields in the YAML frontmatter, then replace the placeholder prompt with specific instructions for your API use case.
